reasoning_trace.finding: Confirmed the repository is Thunderbird for Android/K-9 Mail shared code and uses a white-label architecture. No source-level bug details were present.
Confirmed project structure and contribution references; no direct fault information, but it pointed to legacy K-9 history and architecture docs.
The expected local attachment directory did not exist under the checkout, and a recursive search in the repository found no k9mail-logs.txt file.
Search identified the legacy message view implementation as the relevant path, especially MessageViewFragment.kt for mark-unread handling and MessageViewContainerFragment.kt for swipe navigation.
Confirmed legacy modules may be outside the newer feature/core API-internal split; the bug is in legacy UI code, not a module boundary issue.
Found MessageViewContainerFragment as the ViewPager2 swipe container and MessageViewFragment.onToggleRead() as the toolbar mark-read/unread action.
Found MessageHomeActivity as the Activity entry point implementing MessageViewFragmentListener and MessageViewContainerListener.

The container hosts individual MessageViewFragment pages in ViewPager2, tracks messageReference, updates active message on page callbacks, and exposes onToggleRead() by delegating to the active page fragment.
Normal ViewPager2 page changes call setActiveMessage() only on idle or immediate selected state, which updates messageReference and notifies MessageHomeActivity.

The active page fragment is looked up from adapter.getItemId(messageReference) and childFragmentManager tag f$itemId; if messageReference or adapter state is wrong, toolbar actions can target the wrong page or crash.
MessageViewFragment owns the loaded LocalMessage, reads its SEEN flag for menu state, and toggles SEEN in onToggleRead() via toggleFlag(Flag.SEEN).
onToggleRead() checks the loaded LocalMessage, toggles Flag.SEEN, then optionally performs post-mark-as-unread navigation. This path is correct if invoked on the visible fragment and current message.

toggleFlag() computes newState from message.isSet(flag), calls messagingController.setFlag(account, message.folder.databaseId, listOf(message), flag, newState), updates headers, and invalidates the menu.
markMessageAsOpened() can mark a message as read when it is loaded/resumed, but it is guarded by wasMessageMarkedAsOpened for the active page.
MessageHomeActivity.openMessage() creates MessageViewContainerFragment, wires it to the message list ViewModel, and delegates toolbar actions to the container while in message-view mode.
Post-mark-as-unread navigation either stays on the current message or returns to the list; it does not itself undo the unread flag.
The Activity forwards the active message reference to the message list fragment, which reloads the message list to keep the active message included.

markMessageAsOpened() returns early if already SEEN, otherwise can set Flag.SEEN true and update cache/server. This can explain a read transition on page open, but not a persistent ignored unread action after a completed toggle unless lifecycle/page selection is wrong.
MessageListItem.messageReference is derived from account UUID, folder ID, and message UID; uniqueId is used by ViewPager2 adapter stable IDs.
The message list exposes live data consumed by MessageViewContainerFragment and stores sort override state, but no mark-unread logic is present here.
setActiveMessage() stores the active reference and reloads the message list. The older sort override implementation is commented out, so it is not the direct fault.
The message view toolbar contains R.id.toggle_unread, which maps to MessageViewFragment.onToggleRead(); no alternate action path was found.
The custom swipe sensitivity workaround exists only in MessageViewContainerFragment.kt and attaches an ItemTouchHelper directly to ViewPager2's internal RecyclerView.
This explains mark-unread being ignored only if the container's active page tracking is stale after swipe, because onToggleRead() itself uses the current fragment's loaded LocalMessage correctly.
The statement messagingController.setFlag(account, message.folder.databaseId, listOf(message), flag, newState) is structurally correct and uses the loaded message's own folder/database ID.
This method only chooses whether to stay on the message or return to the list; it does not change flags and cannot directly cause the crash after repeated swipes.
The custom ItemTouchHelper intercepts swipe gestures and changes ViewPager2.currentItem directly, bypassing normal ViewPager2 drag handling and without bounds checks.

For left swipes, onSwiped() executes viewPager.currentItem += 1; for right swipes, it executes viewPager.currentItem -= 1. These statements can select pages outside adapter bounds or desynchronize page state from the container's active message reference.

This can mark newly opened messages read, but it is normal app behavior controlled by settings and does not explain repeated-swipe crash. It is more likely a secondary effect once the wrong page is considered active.

reasoning_trace.target: Fault is the unbounded left-swipe currentItem increment
reasoning_trace.finding: The reported first trigger is swiping left to another message. The exact left-swipe statement can desynchronize or overrun ViewPager2 state; after that, toolbar mark-unread can operate on a stale/non-visible page, and repeated swipes can crash when the adapter/fragment lookup can no longer satisfy the requested page.

fault_candidates.reasoning: This custom ItemTouchHelper is attached to ViewPager2's internal RecyclerView and handles message-view swipes by directly mutating currentItem. The left-swipe path has no bounds check and bypasses the normal ViewPager2 gesture/page-settling flow that the surrounding code relies on to call setActiveMessage() and keep messageReference, activeMessageReference, fragment tags, and toolbar actions synchronized. A stale active reference explains why pressing mark-unread after a swipe can affect the wrong loaded fragment or be overwritten by the visible page's read-on-open lifecycle. Repeated swipes can push currentItem beyond valid adapter positions or leave fragment lookup inconsistent, causing the crash.

final_answer.causal_chain: A message is opened in MessageHomeActivity, which creates MessageViewContainerFragment. The container displays messages in ViewPager2 and expects page callbacks to update messageReference through setActiveMessage(). The custom reduceHorizontalDragSensitivity() workaround attaches ItemTouchHelper to ViewPager2's internal RecyclerView. On a left swipe, the faulty statement directly increments viewPager.currentItem without validating adapter bounds or coordinating with ViewPager2's normal scroll state. This can leave the container's active message reference and the actually visible MessageViewFragment out of sync, so the toolbar mark-unread action can be delegated to a stale fragment or a message whose lifecycle still marks it read. Further swipes can request a page outside the adapter range or make findMessageViewFragment()/adapter item lookup fail, producing the crash described after repeated swipes.
final_answer.fix_hint: Replace the direct currentItem mutation with guarded navigation through showNextMessage()/showPreviousMessage() or explicit bounds-checked setCurrentItem() that keeps setActiveMessage() and fragment state synchronized.
